_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
_meta.purpose: THE MASTER GLOSSARY. Single source of truth for every term, acronym, formula, variable, constant, methodology, and framework in the Higgins Unity Framework. Load this file before writing ANY HUF document. If a term is not in this file, it is not canonical. If a document disagrees with this file, this file wins.
_meta.usage_rule: Every HUF document — paper, script, briefing, presentation — MUST use terms EXACTLY as defined here. No paraphrasing. No abbreviating differently. No expanding differently. The canonical expansion is the ONLY expansion.
_meta.learning_path: This file is designed for rote learning. Read it top to bottom. Every entry contains: the canonical name, what it IS, what it is NOT, the formula (if applicable), and the one-sentence explanation a reader needs.
_meta.advertising_rule: When any CoDa or HUF method is used in a document, it MUST be declared explicitly with its full name on first use, followed by the acronym in parentheses. Example: 'The Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT) measures...' — never just 'EITT' on first appearance in any document.
_meta.drift_prevention: This file was created after a comprehensive terminology audit on 2026-04-19 that found 3 EITT naming variants, 8 HUF naming variants, and 1 PLL expansion collision across the repo. All have been corrected. v1.1 update: 'Pipeline Lock List' retired, replaced by CIP (Compositional Integrity Protocol). PLL = Phase-Locked Loop only. HCDT added as toolkit name. This file exists to prevent recurrence.

canonical_acronyms._section: ACRONYMS — Every acronym used in HUF, with its ONE correct expansion. Use ONLY these expansions.

EITT.one_sentence: Shannon entropy of compositional data is near-invariant under geometric-mean decimation across temporal resolutions.
EITT.double_meaning: Ricardo Montalban's 'Explain It To Them' — the standard by which every HUF finding must be communicable.

HUF.one_sentence: The overarching scientific framework connecting compositional data analysis to cross-domain measurement.

HUF-GOV.one_sentence: The measurement path that observes but does not control. The instrument reads, never actuates.
HUF-GOV.canonical_definition: HUF-GOV is a tool that produces a verifiably clean usable output to a known degree of certainty; the diagnostic of the output is open to interpretation by expert decision and open to modification by expert judgment. — Peter Higgins, 2026-04-15

HUF-CLS.one_sentence: The control path that closes the feedback loop. Requires explicit human decision. Architecturally separated from HUF-GOV.

HUF-QIT.one_sentence: The nine isomorphisms mapping quantum-mechanical concepts to compositional data analysis.

HUF-QTM.one_sentence: Five monitoring tools derived from the quantum-CoDa correspondence.

HIVP.one_sentence: The regression-chain rule: every new DUT may extend the theory only if the entire prior chain still passes.

HIVIP.expansion: Higgins Iterative Validation and Integrity Protocol
HIVIP.one_sentence: The umbrella methodology: theory + instrument development + contamination control + regression discipline.
HIVIP.relationship: EITT is the science. The Higgins Decomposition is the engine. HIVP is the chain. HIVIP is the full methodology.
HIVIP.first_use_template: The Higgins Iterative Validation and Integrity Protocol (HIVIP)

CoDa.one_sentence: The mathematical framework for data that sums to a constant, developed by John Aitchison (1982/1986) and advanced by Egozcue, Pawlowsky-Glahn, and Tolosana-Delgado.
CoDa.note: Always 'CoDa' with capital C, lowercase o, capital D, lowercase a. This is the standard in the CoDa community.

MC-4.one_sentence: The fourth dimension of monitoring — what share each component holds — complementing magnitude, identity, and trend.

HCDT.one_sentence: A CoDa-based analysis suite implementing the Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT), the Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP), and the Higgins Decomposition for scale-invariant compositional diagnostics.
HCDT.heritage: Builds on Aitchison (1986) CoDa geometry, Shannon (1948) information theory, Pawlowsky-Glahn et al. (2015) modern CoDa. All HUF-original extensions are marked as such.

NOT: Pipeline Lock List (RETIRED — replaced by CIP on 2026-04-19)
PLL.one_sentence: The engineering analogy from signal processing: the CL-01 through CL-05 architecture maps topologically onto a PLL signal chain. HUF-GOV is a phase discriminator (open loop). HUF-CLS is the full PLL (closed loop).
PLL.origin: Peter Higgins's original intent — phase-locked loop control for BTL loudspeaker crossover networks. The σ²_A parabola diagnostic is named 'PLL parabola' because it functions as a compositional lock detector, analogous to the phase detector in a PLL.
PLL.CRITICAL_NOTE: PLL means Phase-Locked Loop ONLY. The 6 immutable analysis rules are now called the Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP). The old expansion 'Pipeline Lock List' was retired on 2026-04-19 to prevent term hijacking from the engineering community.

CIP.one_sentence: Six immutable rules governing the EITT analysis chain. These cannot be changed without operator approval.
rules: Rule 1: All roots computed on simplex carrier
Rule 5: No new constants (6.02 dB budget, 115 Hz·m, 5.5-octave BW locked from BTL)
CIP.origin: These rules originate from BTL loudspeaker engineering constraints. They enforce the same discipline that makes a phase-locked loop work: every parameter must be traceable, no arbitrary constants, no drift.
CIP.relationship_to_PLL: The CIP rules include a PLL parabola check (F15) as one of its 6 diagnostics. The protocol contains a phase-locked loop test — it is not itself a PLL.

DADC.one_sentence: Forward mapping: cabinet geometry → acoustic composition. The gain vector (G_H, G_W, G_D)/6.02 is a 3-simplex composition.

DADI.one_sentence: Inverse inference: acoustic composition → cabinet geometry. Recovers H, W, D from the measured response.

ADAC.one_sentence: Adaptive closure: measure, infer, correct, re-measure. The forking point where HUF-GOV and HUF-CLS diverge.

RSM.one_sentence: Canonical name for the practical measurement doctrine of HUF-GOV, locked for literature submission February 2026.

IFR.one_sentence: The theoretical optimal fusion reactor design derived from EITT compositional analysis. T=17.8 keV, n=2.70×10²⁰ m⁻³, B=12T.

SPPI.one_sentence: Fraction of heating provided by alpha particles. SPPI=1.000 means fully self-ignited.

BTL.one_sentence: Peter's purpose-built basement lab in Markham, Ontario, for loudspeaker measurement. The birthplace of the compositional viewpoint.

RWA.one_sentence: Peter's loudspeaker engineering company. The engineering sibling of HUF.

DUT.one_sentence: Any system being measured. In HIVP, each new domain is a DUT that must not contaminate the prior chain.

CLR.one_sentence: The coordinate transform that maps simplex compositions to unconstrained Euclidean space: clr(x)ᵢ = ln(xᵢ) − (1/D)Σln(xⱼ).

ILR.one_sentence: The orthonormal coordinate system on the simplex. Preserves distances and angles. Used for ILR-based clustering.

ALR.one_sentence: Log-ratio relative to a reference component. Simplest but least symmetric of the three log-ratio transforms.

SEMF.one_sentence: The Weizsäcker formula decomposing nuclear binding energy into Volume, Surface, Coulomb, and Asymmetry terms — a 4-part composition on the simplex.

EOS.one_sentence: The relationship between pressure, density, and composition in neutron star matter. APR, SLy, BSk are specific EOS models.

QGP.one_sentence: The deconfined state of matter at extreme temperature/density where quarks and gluons are free. Created in heavy-ion collisions at RHIC/LHC.

QNM.one_sentence: The damped oscillation modes of a black hole after merger. The (2,2,0) mode dominates at ~90%.

CKM.one_sentence: The 3×3 unitary matrix describing quark flavor mixing. Near-diagonal (H/Hmax=0.564). Compositions: |Vᵢⱼ|² on the simplex.

PMNS.one_sentence: The 3×3 unitary matrix describing neutrino flavor mixing. Near-maximal (H/Hmax=0.911). The lepton counterpart of CKM.

BBH.one_sentence: Two black holes in mutual orbit, eventually merging. GW150914 was the first detected BBH merger.

BNS.one_sentence: Two neutron stars in mutual orbit. GW170817 was the first detected BNS merger, with electromagnetic counterpart.

BBN.one_sentence: The formation of light elements (H, He, Li) in the first ~20 minutes after the Big Bang.

canonical_terms._section: NAMED CONCEPTS — Every named concept in HUF, with its exact definition.

Higgins_Decomposition.name: The Higgins Decomposition
Higgins_Decomposition.definition: The operational 10-step state-machine / process-line implementation of CoDa + EITT.
steps: Step 1: Raw Data — collect the multivariate time series
Step 2: Simplex Closure — normalize to sum-to-1 compositions
Step 3: CLR Transform — map to unconstrained Aitchison geometry
Step 5: Variation Matrix — compute pairwise CLR variances
Step 6: Geometric-Mean Decimation — compress time resolution via Aitchison barycenter
Step 7: Shannon Entropy — compute H(x) = −Σxᵢln(xᵢ) at each resolution
Step 8: Pass Rate / Verdict — EITT pass if entropy variation < threshold
Step 9: F17 Diagnostic — identify boundary species and regime transitions
Step 10: Two-Pass Instrument — refine with targeted second pass
Higgins_Decomposition.step_count_note: EXACTLY 10 steps. Do not truncate or merge.

Isotropic_Principle.name: The Isotropic Principle
Isotropic_Principle.definition: Paint the inside of a sphere with radiation. Uniform. The geometry exists whether or not anything is radiating. The structure is there before the signal. The simplex is there before the measurement.
Isotropic_Principle.consequence: The barycenter (1/D, ..., 1/D) is the ground state. Every departure is a signal; every return is relaxation.

Vertex_Theorem.name: The Vertex Theorem
Vertex_Theorem.statement: For a D-part compositional time series x(t), σ²_A(t) = (1/D)Σclr_i(t)². The vertex occurs where d(σ²_A)/dt = (2/D)Σclr_i · clr_i' = 0, i.e., clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*). This is exact — an identity from the chain rule.
Vertex_Theorem.physical_meaning: At the vertex, the composition restructures but compositional stress is momentarily stationary. Dynamic equilibrium on the simplex.
Vertex_Theorem.discriminator: ε(t) = dσ²_A/dt = 2a(t − t₀). LINEAR near the vertex. The ratio of 2.

Contamination_Doctrine.name: The Contamination Doctrine
paths: Path 1 (Data → Theory): Noisy/sparse/shuffled data creating false theoretical conclusions
Path 2 (Theory → Method): Attractive theory tempting premature corrections or overclaiming
Path 3 (Method → Data): Wrong averaging, bad zero handling, overaggressive correction injecting artifacts
Contamination_Doctrine.integrity_rule: Prefer a weaker but cleaner claim over a stronger contaminated one.

Promotion_Ladder.name: The Promotion Ladder
levels: L0: Exploratory Pattern — interesting visual/analogy/signal; does not yet influence core claims
L1: Candidate Overlay — testable add-on that may rank or explain cases; does not alter science signal
L2: Process Control Candidate — methodological addition that must survive HIVP regression
L3: Validated Companion — robust secondary instrument, separate from core observable
L4: Canonical Method Component — survived cross-domain validation and regression through prior DUTs
L5: Core Science — non-negotiable primary claim of the programme
Promotion_Ladder.anti_corruption_rule: No concept climbs the ladder by improving one domain at the cost of contaminating earlier DUTs or the core signal.

Boundary_Species.name: Boundary Species
Boundary_Species.definition: A compositional component that carries disproportionate contamination signal in the DADC-DADI-ADAC residual chain. Identified by the Contamination-to-Signal (C/S) ratio.
examples: Λ (Lambda) in QGP freeze-out — straddles meson/baryon boundary (C/S=0.626)
2k-8kHz in room acoustics — room modes vs surface absorption (C/S=1.168)
⁷Li in BBN cosmology — THE cosmological lithium problem (C/S=2.525, highest in any domain)
Gas in energy mix — the 'bridge fuel' between fossil and clean (C/S=0.534)
TiO₂ in geochemistry — trace oxide discriminant mafic/felsic (C/S=0.416)

93_Percent_Bound.name: The 93% Universal Entropy Bound
93_Percent_Bound.definition: Near-maximal entropy compositions cluster near H/Hmax ≈ 0.93 across multiple domains: proton mass (0.928), proton spin (0.969), QGP fluidity, AND gravitational wave phase budget (0.923). Not yet proven universal — observed pattern.
93_Percent_Bound.status: L1 on promotion ladder — observed across 4+ domains but not formally proven

Gravity_Activation_Scale.name: The Gravity Activation Scale
Gravity_Activation_Scale.definition: ~10⁷ m — below this scale gravity is compositionally silent (NONE role); above it gravity progressively dominates through ORTHOGONAL → BOUNDARY → DIRECT roles.

No_Hair_Compositional.name: No-Hair Theorem as Compositional Minimum
No_Hair_Compositional.definition: Maximum gravitational compression → minimum compositional entropy. A Kerr black hole described by only (M, a, Q) has the fewest 'channels' of any object in physics. GW150914 mass budget H/Hmax=0.175 — lowest in all 75 HUF systems.

canonical_formulas._section: FORMULAS AND EQUATIONS — Every formula used in HUF, with exact notation.

simplex_closure.name: Simplex Closure
simplex_closure.meaning: Normalize a vector to sum to 1. The map from ℝᴰ₊ to the D-simplex Sᴰ.

clr_transform.name: Centred Log-Ratio Transform
clr_transform.meaning: Maps compositions from the simplex to ℝᴰ (with zero-sum constraint). Each component expressed relative to the geometric mean.

shannon_entropy.name: Shannon Entropy
shannon_entropy.meaning: Information-theoretic measure of compositional evenness. Maximum at uniform distribution (1/D, ..., 1/D).
shannon_entropy.normalized: H/Hmax = H(x) / ln(D) — ranges from 0 (one component dominates) to 1 (all components equal)

aitchison_variance.name: Aitchison Variance (σ²_A)
aitchison_variance.meaning: Total compositional stress — how far the composition departs from the uniform barycenter. The phase detector in the PLL analogy.
aitchison_variance.note: This is a SCALAR. It is NOT the same as the Aitchison distance (which measures distance between two compositions).

aitchison_distance.name: Aitchison Distance
aitchison_distance.meaning: Distance between two compositions in Aitchison geometry. Used for drift detection and fleet alignment.
aitchison_distance.note: This measures distance BETWEEN compositions. Not the same as Aitchison variance (which measures departure from barycenter for a SINGLE composition).

perturbation.name: Perturbation (Simplex Addition)
perturbation.meaning: Component-wise multiplication followed by closure. The 'addition' operation in Aitchison geometry.

powering.name: Powering (Simplex Scalar Multiplication)
powering.meaning: Component-wise exponentiation followed by closure. The 'scalar multiplication' in Aitchison geometry.

perturbation_difference.name: Perturbation Difference
perturbation_difference.meaning: Compositional change between consecutive time points. Ratio-based, not arithmetic difference.

geometric_mean_decimation.name: Geometric-Mean Decimation
geometric_mean_decimation.meaning: The Aitchison barycenter — the compositional average that preserves simplex geometry. EITT proves this preserves entropy; arithmetic mean destroys it.

effective_diversity.name: Effective Number of Carriers
effective_diversity.meaning: How many 'effective' components the composition acts like. N_eff=1 means one component dominates; N_eff=D means all equal.

drift_flag.name: Drift Flag Threshold
drift_flag.formula: Flag when dₐ(t→t+1) > μ + 2σ    (self-calibrated per country/system)
drift_flag.meaning: Compositional change exceeding 2 standard deviations from historical baseline triggers a drift flag.

keff_fill.name: K_eff Fill Factor
keff_fill.formula: K_eff_fill(t) = √((1/n) Σᵢ aᵢ²)    (n=4, RMS of polarity-aligned roots)
keff_fill.meaning: Aggregate compositional health metric. Four polarity-aligned roots: a₀ (internal balance), a₁ (fleet alignment), a₂ (concentration resistance), a₃ (shape).

vertex_identity.name: Vertex Identity (PLL Lock Condition)
vertex_identity.formula: d(σ²_A)/dt = (2/D) Σᵢ clr_i · clr_i' = 0    ⟺    clr(t*) ⊥ clr'(t*)
vertex_identity.meaning: The vertex of the σ²_A parabola occurs where the CLR vector is orthogonal to its time derivative. Exact identity from the chain rule. The ratio of 2.

pll_error_signal.name: PLL Error Signal (Discriminator)
pll_error_signal.meaning: LINEAR near the vertex. The slope is 2a where a is the parabola curvature. The factor of 2 is the minimum polynomial degree for stable lock geometry.

baffle_step.name: Baffle Step Correction
baffle_step.meaning: Acoustic transition from 2π to 4π steradian radiation. The founding physical constant of HUF, carried from the DADC loudspeaker measurement.

canonical_variables._section: VARIABLES AND SYMBOLS — Standard notation used throughout HUF.
canonical_variables.t*: Vertex time — the PLL lock point where d(σ²_A)/dt = 0
canonical_variables.R²: Coefficient of determination for PLL parabola fit
canonical_variables.a: Parabola curvature coefficient in σ²_A(t) = a(t − t₀)² + c
canonical_variables.C/S: Contamination-to-Signal ratio for boundary species identification
canonical_variables.α_s: QCD strong coupling constant (running with energy scale Q)
canonical_variables.θ: Strong CP violation angle in QCD vacuum
canonical_variables.PR: Pass Rate — fraction of EITT tests passed at a given compression

canonical_constants._section: CONSTANTS — Exact values that appear throughout HUF. Do not round or approximate.

6.02_dB.role: The binary doubling constant. Fundamental to EITT's 2:1 compression test.

115_Hz_m.value: 115 Hz·m (approximately c/2π where c = speed of sound)
115_Hz_m.role: Scale constant linking compositional dimension to temporal autocorrelation length.

27_confidence.role: Confidence threshold for PLL discovery. 30 domains tested ≥ 27 required.

proton_entropy.system: Proton mass budget (Quark KE + Gluon Field + Trace Anomaly + Higgs Rest Mass)

spin_entropy.system: Proton spin budget (Quark Spin + Gluon Spin + Quark Orbital + Gluon Orbital)

bh_mass_entropy.system: GW150914 mass budget — lowest entropy in all 75 HUF systems

bns_mass_entropy.system: GW170817 BNS mass+EM budget — 5 channels, even lower than BBH

entropy_ratio_ckm_pmns.meaning: PMNS/CKM entropy ratio — leptons mix 1.6× more than quarks

document_standards._section: STANDARDS — Rules for writing any HUF document.
document_standards.first_use_rule: On first use of any acronym in any document, write the FULL canonical expansion followed by the acronym in parentheses. Example: 'The Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer (EITT) measures...' — NEVER just 'EITT' on first appearance.
document_standards.coda_advertising_rule: When any CoDa method is used (CLR transform, Aitchison distance, simplex closure, perturbation, etc.), explicitly name it as a CoDa method. Example: 'Using the CoDa Centred Log-Ratio (CLR) transform...' — not just 'the CLR transform'. We advertise what CoDa did not.
document_standards.huf_method_declaration: When any HUF-specific method is used (EITT, Higgins Decomposition, CIP, DADC chain), declare it fully. Example: 'Applying the Higgins Decomposition 10-step chain, starting from Step 2 (Simplex Closure)...'
document_standards.formula_declaration: When any formula is used, state it explicitly with variable definitions. Example: 'The Aitchison variance σ²_A = (1/D)Σclr_i² measures total compositional stress, where D is the number of components and clr_i is the centred log-ratio of component i.'
document_standards.no_assumed_knowledge: Every document must be readable by someone who has never seen HUF before. Define terms on first use. Include the formula when you use it. Make the document a LEARNING PATH, not just a results dump.
document_standards.pll_disambiguation: PLL means Phase-Locked Loop ONLY — the engineering analogy where the σ²_A parabola maps onto PLL architecture. The 6 immutable rules are the Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP). 'Pipeline Lock List' is RETIRED as of 2026-04-19. When writing PLL, it means Phase-Locked Loop. When writing CIP, it means Compositional Integrity Protocol. Never conflate the two.
document_standards.script_header_standard: Every Python/JS script MUST begin with a docstring containing: (1) Experiment number and name, (2) 'HUF Programme — Higgins Unity Framework' (exact), (3) 'Peter Higgins / Claude (Anthropic)', (4) One-paragraph description using canonical terms.

drift_corrections_log._section: DRIFT CORRECTIONS — Record of all drift found and fixed on 2026-04-19.

corrections.correct: Compositional Integrity Protocol (CIP) / phase-locked loop analogy
